cli.usage.portNote: Without MONAD_PORT the port in config.json (network.port) is used.
cli.usage.remoteNote: To target a remote daemon, configure the connection in the UI client.
cli.cmd.init.desc: initialize monad (interactive setup: home directory + model provider)
cli.cmd.upgrade.desc: check for and apply monad updates
cli.cmd.upgrade.checkFlag: check for updates without applying them
cli.cmd.upgrade.channelFlag: release channel: stable (default), beta, or nightly
cli.cmd.upgrade.pruneFlag: remove all but the 3 most recent binary backups and exit
cli.cmd.upgrade.notesFlag: show release notes alongside version info
cli.cmd.reset.desc: selectively reset parts of the monad system (sessions, config, auth, usage, all)
cli.cmd.purge.desc: wipe and rebuild monad home (destructive — requires double confirmation)
cli.cmd.status.desc: check whether the daemon is running
cli.cmd.logs.desc: show the daemon's logs (-f to follow, -n to set the number of lines)
cli.cmd.doctor.desc: diagnose configuration, connection, and version problems
cli.cmd.chat.desc: talk to your agent (streams the reply; interactive when given no message)
cli.cmd.commands.desc: list available slash commands (built-ins, atom commands, skills)
cli.cmd.completion.desc: output a shell completion script (bash, zsh, fish)
cli.cmd.session.desc: session operations (new, list, send, watch, search, branch, restore, …)
cli.cmd.model.desc: manage model profiles (list, set, rm), the default (use), and connection tests (test)
cli.cmd.provider.desc: manage model providers (list, set, remove) and list their models
cli.cmd.credential.desc: manage provider credentials (list, add, remove, test)
cli.cmd.usage.desc: show cumulative token/cost usage (the global ledger); --reset wipes it
cli.cmd.config.desc: read or write configuration (e.g. monad config set network.transport uds)
cli.cmd.acp.desc: run as an Agent Client Protocol agent on stdio (editor integration, e.g. Zed)
cli.cmd.tls.desc: manage the daemon TLS certificate (show, renew, trust)
cli.tls.usageShow: Show the active certificate fingerprint and expiry.
cli.tls.usageTrust: Trust the local daemon certificate in the macOS login keychain.
cli.tls.usageRestartHint: The daemon must be restarted for a new cert to take effect.
cli.tls.certNotFound: No TLS certificate found. Enable remote access in config to generate one.
cli.tls.restartHint: Restart the daemon for the new certificate to take effect:
cli.tls.trustedHint: Refresh the browser after trusting the certificate.
cli.tls.trustUnsupported: monad tls trust currently supports macOS only. Import the shown cert into your OS/browser trust store manually.
cli.err.forceHint: pass --force to ignore or upgrade to a matching version
cli.reset.warning: This will permanently delete all data in:
cli.reset.lost: Sessions, credentials, model settings, and agent config will be lost.
cli.reset.confirm2: Are you sure? This cannot be undone. [y/N]
cli.reset.restartHint: Run "monad start" to start a fresh daemon.
cli.upgrade.fetchFailed: ✖ failed to fetch release info from GitHub. Check your network and try again.
cli.upgrade.noBackup: ✖ no backup found — nothing to roll back to
cli.cmd.reset.keepLastFlag: for "sessions": keep this many most-recent sessions
cli.resetCmd.sessions.warning: This will delete all session history (SQLite database). Config and credentials are kept.
cli.resetCmd.sessions.keepWarning: This will delete all sessions except the {{n}} most recent.
cli.resetCmd.config.warning: This will reset config.json and profile.json to defaults. Credentials are kept.
cli.resetCmd.config.hint: Run "monad init" to reconfigure, or "monad start" to start with defaults.
cli.resetCmd.auth.warning: This will clear all provider credentials. Config and sessions are kept.
cli.resetCmd.auth.hint: Run "monad init" to add a provider, or configure credentials via the UI.
cli.config.noConfig: no config yet — run {{cmd}} or start the daemon first
cli.usageCmd.title: Usage
cli.console.notReady: ● daemon did not become reachable in time
cli.init.startOver: To start over, run: monad reset
cli.cmd.channel.desc: manage channels and approve pairing requests
cli.channel.addUsage: usage: monad channel add <type> [--label X] [--agent ID] [--policy allowlist|pairing|open|disabled]
cli.channel.addNext: next: monad channel token {{id}} <bot-token>, then monad channel enable {{id}}
cli.channel.paired: approved pairing — user added to allowlist
cli.cmd.peer.desc: manage peer daemons for task delegation
cli.peer.addUsage: usage: monad peer add <base-url> [--label X] [--agent NAME] [--id peer_…]
cli.peer.addNext: next: monad peer token {{id}} <token>  (restart the daemon to apply)
cli.peer.tokenSet: peer token stored (restart the daemon to apply)
cli.atom.installUsage: usage: monad atom install <github:owner/repo@sha | npm:name@ver | local:/path> [--yes]
cli.atom.scaffoldUsage: usage: monad atom scaffold <type> [dir]  (type: a-z0-9- slug, e.g. matrix)
cli.atom.scaffoldNext: next: cd {{dir}} && bun install && bun run build, then `monad atom install local:{{dir}}`
cli.atom.consentHint: re-run with --yes to consent and install
cli.atom.channelHint: add a channel (monad model … / settings) to start using it
cli.model.use.desc: get or set the default model profile
cli.model.testConnection.desc: probe a provider + key without saving it
cli.session.new.desc: create a session, print its id
cli.session.list.desc: list sessions, optionally filtered by state
cli.session.send.desc: send a message (streams the reply; --no-stream for full reply, --detach to fire-and-forget)
cli.session.watch.desc: stream a session's events (Ctrl-C to stop)
cli.session.branch.desc: copy session history into a new independent session
cli.session.restore.desc: rewind a session to a message checkpoint
cli.session.reset.desc: clear all messages from a session, keeping the session itself
cli.session.abort.desc: cancel an in-flight run for a session
cli.session.rm.desc: delete a session and its data
cli.daemon.fixConfig: Fix config.json and run `monad-dev start` again.
cli.daemon.notReady: ● daemon did not become ready in time
cli.win.gitBashMissing: ✖ Git Bash not found — reinstall monad (the installer bundles Git Bash automatically).
cli.browser.notFound: No Playwright browser found — required for the browser tools.
cli.browser.installPrompt: Install Chromium now (npx playwright install chromium)? [Y/n]:
cli.browser.skipped: Skipped — run `npx playwright install chromium` before using browser tools.
cli.browser.installing: Installing Chromium via Playwright… (this can take a minute)
cli.browser.installFailed: ✖ install failed — run `npx playwright install chromium` manually.
cli.skills.usage: usage: monad skills [list | new <name> | validate <path> | install <local-path | git:owner/repo | url> | remove <name>]
cli.skills.editThenValidate: edit the SKILL.md, then run `monad skills validate <path>`
cli.skills.hotReload: a running daemon picks up new skills automatically (hot reload)
cli.skills.none: no skills found — add SKILL.md packets under ~/.monad/skills/<name>/
cli.session.restore.head: head={{id}}
cli.licenses.title: {{count}} packages
cli.envDeps.nodeNotFound: Node.js not found — required for npm-based MCP servers (npx).
cli.envDeps.uvNotFound: uv not found — required for Python-based MCP servers (uvx).
cli.envDeps.installFailed: ✖ Install failed — install it manually and restart monad init.
cli.chat.replHint: type a message · Ctrl-C interrupts a reply, again to exit
cli.mcp.noneInstalled: no MCP servers installed — add one with: monad mcp add <name> <command…>
cli.mcp.authorizing: authorizing {{name}}… (may open a browser, or print a device code)
cli.pair.scanHint: Scan with the monad mobile app:
cli.pair.rotateHint: Or run: monad pair --rotate  to refresh the token
cli.tui.daemonUnreachable: monad-tui: daemon not reachable — start the daemon first
cli.tui.footer.settings: [Ctrl+S] sessions [Ctrl+P] model [up/down] move [a] add [t] test [x] delete [d] default [r] reload [Esc] close
cli.tui.footer.picker: [Ctrl+S] sessions [Ctrl+P] model [up/down/j/k] move [Enter] open [N] new [R] retry [Esc] close
cli.tui.emptyTranscript: awaiting input… no messages in current buffer
cli.tui.model.title: [ model registry ]
cli.tui.shell.resize: Resize to at least 60 columns × 18 rows.
cli.tui.shell.helpGlobal: Ctrl+K commands · Ctrl+, settings · Ctrl+` workspace · Ctrl+1…9 section
cli.tui.shell.helpFocus: Tab/Shift+Tab focus · arrows or j/k move · Enter activate · Esc back · [ sidebar
cli.tui.shell.helpComposer: Composer: Enter send · Shift+Enter/Ctrl+J newline · Ctrl+C stop
cli.tui.shell.helpMouse: Mouse: click focus/open · wheel list · drag sidebar · Shift+drag selects terminal text
cli.tui.composer.busyHint: Enter queue · Ctrl+Enter steer · Ctrl+C stop{{queued}}
cli.tui.projection.title: External agent projection
cli.tui.projection.empty: No external-agent activity in this session.
cli.tui.projection.readOnly: read-only · h/PageUp history · provider output is normalized by daemon
cli.tui.projection.historyUnavailable: History is unavailable for this runtime.
